rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 21, 2023, line 1. DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S PETITION. OFF CALENDAR. The proof of service on file signed by Alyssa Kern under the penalty of perjury asserts that the Demurrer, dated by counsel on May 23, 2023, was mailed to the Plaintiff on May 9, 2023. Other parties were served on May 23, 2023 and the motion had not been filed until June 5, 2023 for a hearing on June 21, 2023. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARIYA LYUBLINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Naifeh Azars Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration As To The Courts Order Dated May 2, 2023 As To The Sustaining Of Claudio A. Bluers Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 21, 2023, line 2. PLAINTIFF NAIFEH AZAR's Motion For Reconsideration As To The Courts Order Dated May 2, 2023 As To The Sustaining Of Claudio A. Bluers Demurrer To Plaintiffs Complaint. DENIED. rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. LAKRISHA HARPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 21, 2023, line 10. DEFENDANT LAKRISHA HARPER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. Summary Judgment is framed by the pleadings. Defendant doesn't point to any part of the pleadings (complaint or answer) raising the sole issue addressed by the motion, failure to provide VAWA notification. Furthermore, Defendant failed to show that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law or that there are no triable issues of fact regarding Plaintiff's obligation to comply with VAWA notification. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FUNG LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 21, 2023, line 3. DEFENDANT ANNIE O'KEEFFE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that defendant is entitled to a judgement as a matter of law. Plaintiff's objection to request for judicial notice per Cal. Evidence Code 452(d) is sustained. Furthermore, if defendants properly introduced Exhibit 1 as evidence, there would be a triable issue of fact regarding the service of the then current (February 1, 2023) Form 1007 available from the San Francisco Rent Board. (Lee Declaration, paragraph 6). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
